title: An old man who weaves baskets
summary: Describe an elderly basket-weaver and what his work looks like every morning.
topic: An elderly basket-weaver at work in his garden
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences describing an elderly person who does a craft by hand every day. Say where they work, what they use, and what the work looks and sounds like. Use present simple and adverbs of frequency.
hintStarters: Every morning, the old man sits in his small garden and…
He uses thin strips of willow that he…
People in the village sometimes stop to watch because…

hintVocab.meaningVi: cây liễu / cành liễu (dùng để đan)
giết thời gian, cho qua thời gian

sampleText: Every morning, just after sunrise, an old man named Benedikt sits in his garden in a small village in rural Slovakia. In front of him is a bucket of water with thin willow strips soaking inside. He takes one strip at a time and bends it between his fingers to test if it is flexible enough. When a strip is ready, he weaves it over and under the framework of the basket in his lap. His fingers move slowly but without any hesitation. The strips creak softly as he tightens them into place. He usually makes two baskets a day. Sometimes he makes three if the weather is good and his hands do not hurt. Each basket takes about two hours. He never measures anything. He just knows from experience when the shape is right. His baskets are sturdy and last for many years. Farmers in the area often buy them for collecting potatoes and apples at harvest time. Benedikt learned the craft from his uncle when he was twelve years old. He is now eighty-one and still works without glasses. He says the work is not difficult, but you need to be patient. He does not weave to earn money. He weaves because his hands feel wrong when they are still.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ngay sau khi mặt trời mọc

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a bucket of water with thin willow strips soaking inside
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một xô nước với những sợi liễu mỏng đang ngâm bên trong
uốn cong nó giữa các ngón tay

sampleAnnotations.phrase: weaves it over and under the framework
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đan nó qua lại trên khung

sampleAnnotations.phrase: creak softly as he tightens them
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: kêu cót két nhẹ khi ông xiết chặt chúng
Ông không bao giờ đo lường gì
khi hình dạng đúng như ý
vẫn làm việc mà không cần kính

sampleAnnotations.phrase: his hands feel wrong when they are still
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đôi tay ông cảm thấy lạ khi không làm gì

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for habitual action
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng present simple để mô tả những điều ai đó làm thường xuyên hay mỗi ngày. Trong bài: 'He takes one strip at a time', 'He usually makes two baskets a day', 'He never measures anything'. Đây là những hành động lặp lại đều đặn, không phải đang xảy ra ngay lúc này.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use present simple for things someone does regularly or every day. From the sample: 'He takes one strip at a time', 'He usually makes two baskets a day', 'He never measures anything'. These are repeated habits, not actions happening right now.

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi buổi sáng, ngay sau khi mặt trời mọc, một ông lão tên Benedikt ngồi trong vườn nhà ở một ngôi làng nhỏ thuộc vùng nông thôn Slovakia. Trước mặt ông là một xô nước với những sợi liễu mỏng đang ngâm bên trong. Ông lấy từng sợi một và uốn cong giữa các ngón tay để kiểm tra xem nó có đủ dẻo không. Khi một sợi đã sẵn sàng, ông đan nó qua lại trên khung của cái giỏ trên đùi. Các ngón tay ông cử động chậm nhưng không do dự chút nào. Những sợi liễu kêu cót két nhẹ khi ông xiết chặt chúng vào chỗ. Ông thường làm hai cái giỏ mỗi ngày. Đôi khi ông làm ba nếu thời tiết tốt và tay ông không đau. Mỗi cái giỏ mất khoảng hai tiếng. Ông không bao giờ đo lường gì. Ông chỉ biết từ kinh nghiệm khi nào hình dạng đúng như ý. Những cái giỏ của ông rất chắc chắn và dùng được nhiều năm. Nông dân trong vùng thường mua để đựng khoai tây và táo vào mùa thu hoạch. Benedikt học nghề từ người chú khi ông mười hai tuổi. Ông nay tám mươi mốt tuổi và vẫn làm việc mà không cần kính. Ông nói công việc không khó, nhưng bạn cần phải kiên nhẫn. Ông không đan để kiếm tiền. Ông đan vì đôi tay cảm thấy lạ khi không làm gì.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence about when Benedikt works.
options: Every morning, just after sunrise, he sits in his garden.
Every morning, just after sunrise, he is sitting in his garden.
Every morning, just after sunrise, he sit in his garden.
Every mornings, just after sunrise, he sits in his garden.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple ('sits') is used for daily routines. 'Mornings' as a time expression does not take a plural 's' with 'every'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense describes a regular morning habit? Can 'every' go with a plural noun?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes testing whether something is flexible?
options: He bends it to test if it is flexible enough.
He bends it to test if it is enough flexible.
He bends it to test if it is flexible sufficient.
He bends it to test if it is too flexible.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Flexible enough' is the correct word order: adjective + 'enough'. 'Enough' always follows the adjective it modifies.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'enough' sit relative to the adjective it modifies?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the adverb of frequency used correctly in a sentence about daily output.
options: He usually makes two baskets a day.
He makes usually two baskets a day.
Usually, he makes two baskets in a day.
He makes two baskets usually a day.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverbs of frequency go before the main verb. 'He usually makes' is correct. 'In a day' (option C) changes the meaning slightly; 'a day' is the standard frequency phrase.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'usually' go in a positive statement with a main verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'from experience'.
options: He knows from experience when the shape is right.
He knows from experience when is the shape right.
He knows of experience when the shape is right.
He knows by experience when is the shape right.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'From experience' is the correct phrase. The subordinate clause ('when the shape is right') uses standard statement word order, not question order.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition goes with 'experience' in this phrase? What order follows 'when' in a statement?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means 'strong and able to hold a lot of weight'?
options: damp
tight
flexible
sturdy
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Sturdy' means strong and well-made. 'Damp' = slightly wet; 'tight' = firmly fixed; 'flexible' = able to bend.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word means strong and solidly built?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that correctly describes something Benedikt never does.
options: He never measures anything.
He never measure anything.
He doesn't never measure anything.
He never measures something.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Never' with a positive verb ('measures') creates a negative meaning. 'Doesn't never' is a double negative. 'Something' is used in positive sentences; 'anything' is used with negatives.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'never' need a negative auxiliary? What pronoun follows a negative statement?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct way to say that someone learned a skill as a child.
options: He learned the craft from his uncle when he was twelve.
He learned the craft from his uncle when he has twelve.
He learned the craft of his uncle when he was twelve.
He learned the craft by his uncle when he was twelve.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Learn from' is the correct collocation for a teacher/mentor. 'He was twelve' uses past simple for age. 'He has twelve' is wrong ('was' is needed).
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition follows 'learn' when naming the person you learned from?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that explains why Benedikt weaves.
options: He weaves because his hands feel wrong when they are still.
He weaves because his hands feel wrong when they are quiet.
He weaves because his hands feel wrong when they do nothing.
He weaves because his hands feel bad when they do not move.
mcQuestions.explanation: Option A is the most precise and idiomatic: 'feel wrong when they are still' means the hands are uncomfortable when motionless. 'Still' meaning 'not moving' is the most natural choice.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word most naturally describes hands that are not moving?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says farmers buy baskets for a specific use?
options: Farmers often buy them for collecting potatoes at harvest time.
Farmers often buy them for collect potatoes at harvest time.
Farmers often buy them to collect potatoes at harvest time.
Farmers often buy them for to collect potatoes at harvest time.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'For + gerund' and 'to + infinitive' both express purpose, but 'to collect' (option C) uses the standard purpose infinitive after a verb like 'buy'. 'For collect' is wrong; 'for to' is nonstandard.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'buy', which structure expresses purpose: 'for + gerund' or 'to + infinitive'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence with the correct use of 'take' for duration.
options: Each basket takes about two hours.
Each basket is taking about two hours.
Each basket takes about two hour.
Each baskets takes about two hours.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Takes' is the correct present simple third-person singular. 'Two hours' must be plural. 'Each basket' is singular, so 'takes' (not 'take') is correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'each basket' singular or plural? Is 'two hours' always plural?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means 'to put something in water to soften it'?
options: to wash
to soak
to press
to rinse
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Soak' means to leave something in liquid for a period of time so it absorbs the liquid or becomes softer.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word means to leave something in water for a time so it becomes soft?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Benedikt says he weaves because his hands feel wrong when they are still. Do you have any activity that feels the same way — something you do not for money or duty, but because not doing it feels stranger than doing it?
